# redis Provider

The Redis provider manages data inside a [Redis](https://redis.io) instance. It is intended for use cases where Redis acts as a configuration store or cache that is seeded from Terraform — for example, populating feature flags, configuration maps, or lookup sets that other services consume at runtime.

## Example Usage

```terraform
provider "redis" {
  addr = "localhost:6379"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->

## Schema

### Optional

- `addr` (String) Redis server address in `host:port` form. Falls back to the `REDIS_ADDR` environment variable, then `localhost:6379`.
- `db` (Number) Redis database index (0–15). Defaults to `0`.
- `password` (String, Sensitive) Redis password. Falls back to the `REDIS_PASSWORD` environment variable.
- `username` (String) Redis username (Redis 6+ ACL). Falls back to the `REDIS_USERNAME` environment variable.
